translation: Glory be to Allah
reward: Whoever says 'SubhanAllah' 100 times a day, his sins are wiped away even if they were as much as the foam of the sea.

translation: All praise is for Allah
reward: Alhamdulillah fills the scales of good deeds (Mizan). [Sahih Muslim 223]

translation: Allah is the Greatest
reward: Saying SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah and Allahu Akbar 33 times each after every prayer is more beloved to me than all that there is under the sun. [Sahih Muslim 597]

translation: There is no god but Allah
reward: Whoever says it 100 times will have a reward equivalent to freeing 10 slaves, 100 good deeds written and 100 sins wiped, and protection from Shaytan for the rest of the day. [Bukhari 3293]

translation: I seek forgiveness from Allah
reward: The Prophet ﷺ used to seek forgiveness more than 70 times a day. Whoever does Istighfar regularly, Allah will relieve him of every distress and provide for him from where he cannot imagine. [Abu Dawud 1518]

translation: May Allah's peace and blessings be upon him
reward: Whoever sends blessings upon me once, Allah will send blessings upon him ten times. [Sahih Muslim 408]

translation: We have entered the morning and at this very time all dominion belongs to Allah
reward: Said every morning, it is from the Sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ. Brings protection and barakah for the day. [Abu Dawud 5071]

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ النُّشُورُ
transliteration: Allahumma bika asbahna wa bika amsayna wa bika nahya wa bika namutu wa ilaikan-nushur
translation: O Allah, by You we enter the morning, by You we enter the evening, by You we live and by You we die, and unto You is the Resurrection
reward: A comprehensive morning supplication from the Sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ. [Tirmidhi 3391]

arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
translation: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of all existence
reward: Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi in the morning will be protected from Shaytan until the evening. [Nasai, Hakim]

translation: Glory be to Allah and praise be to Him
reward: Whoever says it 100 times in the morning and evening, no one will come on the Day of Judgment with something better — except one who said it more. [Bukhari 6405, Muslim 2691]

translation: We have entered the evening, and at this very time all dominion belongs to Allah
reward: Evening counterpart of the morning Sunnah dhikr. Brings protection and barakah through the night. [Abu Dawud 5071]

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ
transliteration: Allahumma bika amsayna wa bika asbahna wa bika nahya wa bika namutu wa ilaykal-masir
translation: O Allah, by You we enter the evening, by You we enter the morning, by You we live and by You we die, and to You is the final return
reward: Complete evening supplication from the Sunnah. [Tirmidhi 3391]

translation: Glory be to Allah and praise be to Him
reward: Whoever says it 100 times in the morning and evening, no one will come on the Day of Judgment with something better. [Bukhari 6405]

translation: Glory be to Allah
reward: After every obligatory prayer: 33 SubhanAllah, 33 Alhamdulillah, 33 Allahu Akbar, then La ilaha illallah once — sins are forgiven even if they are as much as the foam of the sea. [Muslim 597]

translation: All praise is for Allah
reward: Said 33 times after every obligatory prayer. Fills the scales on the Day of Judgment. [Sahih Muslim 223]

translation: Allah is the Greatest
reward: Said 34 times after every obligatory prayer. Together with SubhanAllah and Alhamdulillah, these 100 tasbihat complete the post-prayer dhikr. [Bukhari, Muslim]

arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
translation: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of all existence
reward: Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi after every obligatory prayer is guaranteed Paradise when they die. [Nasai, authenticated by Ibn Hibban]

arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
translation: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of all existence
reward: Recite Ayat al-Kursi before sleeping and a guardian from Allah will protect you, and Shaytan will not come near you until morning. [Bukhari 2311]

translation: Glory be to Allah
reward: Before sleeping: 33 SubhanAllah, 33 Alhamdulillah, 34 Allahu Akbar — this is better for you than a servant. [Bukhari 3113]

translation: All praise is for Allah
reward: Part of the bedtime tasbih prescribed by the Prophet ﷺ to Fatimah and Ali (ra). [Bukhari 3113]

translation: Allah is the Greatest
reward: Part of the bedtime tasbih. The Prophet ﷺ said this is better for you than having a servant. [Bukhari 3113]

translation: In Your name, O Allah, I die and I live
reward: Recited before sleeping. The Prophet ﷺ used to say this every night before sleeping. [Bukhari 6312]
